devanagari: मुञ्जाटव्यां गवां गोपानां च दावानलाद् रक्षणम् । श्रीशुक उवाच । क्रीडासक्तेषु गोपेषु तद्‍गावो दूरचारिणीः । स्वैरं चरन्त्यो विविशुः तृणलो...
english_devnagari: muñjāṭavyāṃ gavāṃ gopānāṃ ca dāvānalād rakṣaṇamśrīśuka uvācakrīḍāsakteṣu gopeṣu tad‍gāvo dūracāriṇīḥsvairaṃ carantyo viviśuḥ tṛṇalobhena gahvaram
translation:
Śrī Śuka continued:
While the cowherds were deeply engrossed in playing, their cows roamed to a great distance, grazing at will, and lured by green pasture, strayed into an inaccessible part of the dense jungle.

devanagari: अजा गावो महिष्यश्च निर्विशन्त्यो वनाद् वनम् । ईषीकाटवीं निर्विविशुः क्रन्दन्त्यो दावतर्षिताः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: ajā gāvo mahiṣyaśca nirviśantyo vanād vanamīṣīkāṭavīṃ nirviviśuḥ krandantyo dāvatarṣitāḥ
translation: Entering from one forest (-area) to another, the she- goats, cows and she-buffaloes became thirsty while wandering in the forest, began to cry loudly and got into a thicket of reeds.

devanagari: तेऽपश्यन्तः पशून् गोपाः कृष्णरामादयस्तदा । जातानुतापा न विदुः विचिन्वन्तो गवां गतिम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: te'paśyantaḥ paśūn gopāḥ kṛṣṇarāmādayastadājātānutāpā na viduḥ vicinvanto gavāṃ gatim
translation: Not finding the cattle, cowherds, of whom Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma were prominent, felt repentance (for their dereliction of duty). Despite their search for the cows, they were at a loss to know where they could have gone.

devanagari: तृणैस्तत्खुरदच्छिन्नैः गोष्पदैः अंकितैर्गवाम् । मार्गं अन्वगमन् सर्वे नष्टाजीव्या विचेतसः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: tṛṇaistatkhuradacchinnaiḥ goṣpadaiḥ aṃkitairgavāmmārgaṃ anvagaman sarve naṣṭājīvyā vicetasaḥ
translation: Having thus lost their means of livelihood, the cowherds got perplexed not knowing what to do. Then all of them followed the track of the cows marked with their footprints and blades of grass cut by their hoofs.

devanagari: मुञ्जाटव्यां भ्रष्टमार्गं क्रन्दमानं स्वगोधनम् । सम्प्राप्य तृषिताः श्रान्ताः ततस्ते संन्यवर्तयन् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: muñjāṭavyāṃ bhraṣṭamārgaṃ krandamānaṃ svagodhanamsamprāpya tṛṣitāḥ śrāntāḥ tataste saṃnyavartayan
translation:
Finding their wealth of cattle strayed from its path in the jungle of Muñja-grass and lowing helplessly, they directed their herds back and were overwhelmed with fatigue and thirst.
[Bhāgavata Candrikā’s text adds:]
5(A). Climbing up to the top of a tall tree and showing his personality dark like a cloud in complexion, Kṛṣṇa loudly called out those distressed cows by their individual names.

devanagari: ता आहूता भगवता मेघगम्भीरया गिरा । स्वनाम्नां निनदं श्रुत्वा प्रतिनेदुः प्रहर्षिताः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: tā āhūtā bhagavatā meghagambhīrayā girāsvanāmnāṃ ninadaṃ śrutvā pratineduḥ praharṣitāḥ
translation: Being called by their names by the glorious Lord in a tone deep like the thundering of clouds, the cows became overwhelmed with joy to hear the sound of their names, and responded with lowing loudly.

devanagari: ततः समन्ताद् वनधूमकेतुः । यदृच्छयाभूत् क्षयकृत् वनौकसाम् । समीरितः सारथिनोल्बणोल्मुकैः । विलेलिहानः स्थिरजङ्गमान् महान् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ samantād vanadhūmaketuḥyadṛcchayābhūt kṣayakṛt vanaukasāmsamīritaḥ sārathinolbaṇolmukaiḥvilelihānaḥ sthirajaṅgamān mahān
translation: Thereafter, as the Fate would have it, there broke out, on all side, a forest conflagration causing havoc in all the inhabitants of the forest. And helped by its assistant, the strong wind, the great fire appeared desirous of lapping up all the mobile and immobile creatures with its tongues of dreadful flames.

devanagari: तं आपतन्तं परितो दवाग्निं । गोपाश्च गावः प्रसमीक्ष्य भीताः । ऊचुश्च कृष्णं सबलं प्रपन्ना । यथा हरिं मृत्युभयार्दिता जनाः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ āpatantaṃ parito davāgniṃgopāśca gāvaḥ prasamīkṣya bhītāḥūcuśca kṛṣṇaṃ sabalaṃ prapannāyathā hariṃ mṛtyubhayārditā janāḥ
translation: Observing the advance of the forest-fire on all sides, the cowherds and cows got horrified. Just as people afflicted with the fear of death seek asylum with Lord Hari, they resorted to Kṛṣṇa along with Balarām, for shelter and prayed to Him.

devanagari: कृष्ण कृष्ण महावीर हे रामामित विक्रम । दावाग्निना दह्यमानान् प्रपन्नान् त्रातुमर्हथः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa mahāvīra he rāmāmita vikramadāvāgninā dahyamānān prapannān trātumarhathaḥ
translation: “Oh Kṛṣṇa! Oh Kṛṣṇa of infinite prowess! Oh Balarāma! Balarāma of immeasurable power! It now behoves you to protect us who are being burnt by fire, and have approached you for asylum.

devanagari: नूनं त्वद्‍बान्धवाः कृष्ण न चार्हन्त्यवसीदितुम् । वयं हि सर्वधर्मज्ञ त्वन्नाथाः त्वत्परायणाः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: nūnaṃ tvad‍bāndhavāḥ kṛṣṇa na cārhantyavasīditumvayaṃ hi sarvadharmajña tvannāthāḥ tvatparāyaṇāḥ
translation: We are really your kinsmen and as such, should not be destroyed thus. You are the knower of all that is righteousness. We are all devoted to you and have in you our saviour.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । वचो निशम्य कृपणं बन्धूनां भगवान् हरिः । निमीलयत मा भैष्ट लोचनानीत्यभाषत ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācavaco niśamya kṛpaṇaṃ bandhūnāṃ bhagavān hariḥnimīlayata mā bhaiṣṭa locanānītyabhāṣata
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Hearing the pitiful appeal of his cowherd-comrades, Lord Hari assured them, “Don’t be afraid. Please close your eyes.”

devanagari: तथेति मीलिताक्षेषु भगवान् अग्निमुल्बणम् । पीत्वा मुखेन तान्कृच्छ्राद् योगाधीशो व्यमोचयत् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tatheti mīlitākṣeṣu bhagavān agnimulbaṇampītvā mukhena tānkṛcchrād yogādhīśo vyamocayat
translation: With the words ‘Be it so’, they closed their eyes when, in the meanwhile, Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Master of Yoga swallowed up that menacing conflagration and completely saved them from that calamity.

devanagari: ततश्च तेऽक्षीण्युन्मील्य पुनर्भाण्डीरमापिताः । निशाम्य विस्मिता आसन् आत्मानं गाश्च मोचिताः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: tataśca te'kṣīṇyunmīlya punarbhāṇḍīramāpitāḥniśāmya vismitā āsan ātmānaṃ gāśca mocitāḥ
translation: Then, on opening their eyes, they were extremely surprised to find themselves and their cows safe from danger, and brought over to Bhāṇḍīra[1].
purport:
Footnote [1]:
From the details given in VT. about the bunyan tree called Bhāṇḍīra, it appears that at the time of Jīva Gosvāmī. they located it to the Northwest of the Śiva temple called ‘Spāro’ (?) near Vṛndāvana, and the jungle of muñja grass where the conflagration was swallowed up by Kṛṣṇa was ten miles to the south of the bunyan tree.—Bhāgavata Purāṇa 10.19.13 (Vrindabana Edn Vol. I P. 673)

devanagari: कृष्णस्य योगवीर्यं तद् योगमायानुभावितम् । दावाग्नेरात्मनः क्षेमं वीक्ष्य ते मेनिरेऽमरम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇasya yogavīryaṃ tad yogamāyānubhāvitamdāvāgnerātmanaḥ kṣemaṃ vīkṣya te menire'maram
translation: Observing that it was due to the yogic power of Kṛṣṇa and the manifestation of his Yogic Potency (yogamāyā) that they were delivered from (death from) the forest fire, they came to believe in his divine nature.

devanagari: गाः सन्निवर्त्य सायाह्ने सहरामो जनार्दनः । वेणुं विरणयन् गोष्ठं अगाद् गोपैरभिष्टुतः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: gāḥ sannivartya sāyāhne saharāmo janārdanaḥveṇuṃ viraṇayan goṣṭhaṃ agād gopairabhiṣṭutaḥ
translation: Tending homewards the cows in the evening, Kṛṣṇa along with Balarāma, returned to Vraja, playing on his flute, while the cowherd boys were singing his praise.

devanagari: गोपीनां परमानन्द आसीद् गोविन्ददर्शने । क्षणं युगशतमिव यासां येन विनाभवत् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: gopīnāṃ paramānanda āsīd govindadarśanekṣaṇaṃ yugaśatamiva yāsāṃ yena vinābhavat
translation:
The very sight of Kṛṣṇa (Govinda) filled the Gopīs (cowherd-women) with Supreme delight, as every moment of separation from him was felt by them as a hundred ages (yugas).
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे पूर्वार्धे एकोनविंशोऽध्यायः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃsaṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe pūrvārdhe ekonaviṃśo'dhyāyaḥ
